title: WOS Kernel Document
description: A WOS Kernel Document per the Workflow Orchestration Standard (WOS) Kernel Specification v1.0. The kernel is the minimal orchestration substrate: it defines lifecycle topology (states, transitions, events, milestones), case state (typed data with append-only mutation history), actor model (human and system, extensible via actorExtension seam), impact level classification, contract validation interface, provenance Facts tier, durable execution guarantees, and five named extension seams. The kernel is self-sufficient -- a kernel-only deployment orchestrates workflows without requiring any governance layer. WOS is a companion framework to Formspec v1.0 that MUST NOT alter core Formspec processing semantics.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosKernel.description: WOS Kernel specification version marker. MUST be the literal string '1.0'. This marker is what identifies the surrounding JSON object as a WOS Kernel Document and pins it to the 1.0 line of the Kernel Specification; a processor selects the schema and evaluation semantics from this marker alone. Every Kernel Document MUST carry this key at the root (see Kernel §1, §2).

x-lm.intent: Document type marker and version pin for WOS Kernel documents

url.description: Canonical URI identifier for this Kernel Document. The URL names the workflow definition, not any particular instance — all versions of the same workflow SHOULD share this URL, and the pair (url, version) SHOULD be globally unique. Processors use this identifier for cross-document references (governance, correspondence metadata, integration profiles) and for instance-version pinning under Kernel §9.6. Prefer stable, resolvable URIs under a namespace the authoring organization controls.

x-lm.intent: Stable identifier of the workflow across versions

version.description: Version string for this Kernel Document. Semantic Versioning (SemVer 2.0.0) is RECOMMENDED so that processors and governance layers can reason about compatibility. Instance versioning (Kernel §9.6) pins each running workflow instance to the (url, version) pair of the definition that created it.

title.description: Short human-readable name for this workflow, suitable for lists, navigation, and audit summaries. Not interpreted by the processor.

description.description: Free-text description of this workflow's purpose, scope, and intended audience. Consumed by documentation tooling and LLM authoring agents; not interpreted by the processor.
examples: Internal workflow for approving purchase orders under $50k; routed to a manager for approval or rejection.
Medicaid eligibility redetermination with appeal branch; rights-impacting, requires full due process.

status.description: Publication status of this workflow definition. 'draft' means the definition is being authored and SHOULD NOT instantiate production cases. 'active' means the definition is in effect and new instances MAY be created against it. 'retired' means no new instances SHOULD be created, though existing pinned instances continue executing. Transitions move forward: draft → active → retired.

impactLevel.description: Consequence level classification for decisions made within this workflow (Kernel S6). 'rights-impacting': decisions affect individual legal rights -- full due process required (Layer 1). 'safety-impacting': decisions affect safety -- full due process required. 'operational': organizational operations -- due process RECOMMENDED. 'informational': no binding decisions -- due process OPTIONAL. Default: 'operational'.

x-lm.intent: Proportionality index that governs the strength of governance controls across all layers

actors.description: Declarations of actors participating in this workflow (Kernel S3). The kernel defines human and system actor types. Additional types are registered via the actorExtension seam.

x-lm.intent: Declares who participates in the workflow and their provenance requirements

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle topology defining states, transitions, events, and milestones (Kernel S4). The lifecycle is a deterministic pure function: (current states x event x guards -> next states).

x-lm.intent: The statechart governing workflow instance progression

caseFile.description: Typed data schema for the case state (Kernel S5). Case state is an append-only log independent of lifecycle state.

contracts.description: Named contract references for typed data exchanges in this workflow (Kernel §11). Each key is a contract name referenced from `createTask` actions via `contractRef`; each value is a ContractReference declaring the binding type (Formspec or JSON Schema) and the document URI. Contracts are the kernel's abstract validation interface — the processor delegates actual validation to a conformant contract processor.

provenance.description: Provenance configuration for the Facts tier (Kernel S8).

evaluationMode.description: Determines when the processor evaluates transition guards (Runtime Companion S10). 'event-driven' (default): guards are evaluated only when an explicit event arrives. 'continuous': after any case state mutation, the processor re-evaluates all guards in the current configuration -- if any guard that was previously false now evaluates to true, the corresponding transition fires. Continuous mode imposes a convergence cap of 100 re-evaluation cycles per triggering mutation.

x-lm.intent: Controls whether the workflow reacts only to events or also to data changes

maxRelationshipEventDepth.description: Maximum cascade depth for relationship-triggered events (`$related.*`) before the processor halts further generation (Kernel §4.10). A state change in case A may trigger a `$related.*` event in case B (depth 1), in case C (depth 2), and so on; beyond this cap the processor records a `relationshipDepthCapReached` provenance entry and stops. Prevents unbounded cascading in cyclically related cases. Default: 3.

custodyHook.description: Custody posture declaration seam (Kernel §10.5). The kernel treats this content as opaque — the concrete shape is supplied by the binding. A monolithic binding typically declares a single posture (e.g., who may read content, whether recovery requires the user, whether delegated compute exposes content); a distributed binding such as Trellis binds a full Trust Profile object here. When Governance (Layer 1) is adopted, changes to this object are recorded as canonical lifecycle facts.

id.description: Unique identifier for this actor declaration within the workflow.

type.description: Actor type (Kernel S3). 'human': a person who performs tasks and exercises judgment -- provenance requires identity, role, timestamp. 'system': a deterministic software component -- provenance requires component identifier, version, timestamp. Additional types registered via actorExtension seam.

x-lm.intent: Determines provenance requirements and trust boundary placement

description.description: Free-text description of this actor's role in the workflow (e.g., which tasks they perform, what decisions they make). Consumed by documentation and audit tooling; not interpreted by the processor.
examples: Medical reviewer who evaluates clinical documentation

initialState.description: Identifier of the state where execution begins when a new workflow instance is created. MUST match a key in the sibling `states` map. The entry point drives onEntry actions of the initial state (Kernel §4.1, §4.7) and pins the starting active configuration used by the deterministic evaluation algorithm (§4.2).

x-lm.intent: Entry point for the workflow statechart

states.description: Map of state identifiers to State definitions (Kernel §4.3). At least two states (the initial and at least one final) are needed for a meaningful workflow. Keys are state identifiers referenced from transitions' `target`, compound `initialState`, and the lifecycle-level `initialState`. The map defines the complete statechart topology; a Kernel Complete processor rejects documents whose transitions reference undeclared keys.

x-lm.intent: The complete set of states in the workflow statechart

milestones.description: Named conditions on case state that indicate meaningful progress (Kernel §4.13). Keys are milestone identifiers; values are Milestone objects whose FEL `condition` is evaluated against case state. Milestones are observable — they surface in provenance and audit views — but they do NOT affect lifecycle state directly. Use milestones when a caller needs to ask "has the case crossed this point?" without introducing a lifecycle branch.

allOf.$comment: Kernel S4.3: compound states MUST have initialState and a non-empty states map.
Kernel S4.3, S4.4: parallel states MUST have a non-empty regions map.
Kernel S4.3: atomic states MUST NOT carry compound/parallel structural fields.
Kernel S4.3: final states are completion indicators and MUST NOT carry compound/parallel structural fields.
Kernel S4.4: cancellationPolicy is only meaningful on parallel states.
Kernel S4.14: historyState is only meaningful on compound states.

type.description: State type (Kernel S4.3). 'atomic': no substates. 'compound': contains substates with initialState. 'parallel': contains concurrent regions. 'final': completion indicator, no outgoing transitions.

x-lm.intent: Determines the structural behavior of this state in the statechart

onEntry.description: Actions executed when this state is entered.

onExit.description: Actions executed when this state is exited.

transitions.description: Outgoing transitions from this state. Evaluated in document order -- first matching guard wins (Kernel S4.6).

items.description: One semantic tag identifier on this state. Tags are free-form lowercase strings used by governance layers to attach rules via the `lifecycleHook` seam (Kernel §4.12). Conventionally recognized values include `determination`, `review`, `adverse-decision`, `quality-check`, `intake`, `appeal`, `notification`, and `hold`.

tags.description: Semantic tags for governance attachment via lifecycleHook (Kernel §4.12). Conventional tags: determination, review, adverse-decision, quality-check, intake, appeal, notification, hold.

initialState.description: Initial substate identifier for a compound state (Kernel §4.3). REQUIRED when `type` = 'compound'; MUST NOT appear on atomic, parallel, or final states (enforced structurally by the allOf block on this State). When the enclosing compound state is entered, execution proceeds to this substate unless a `historyState` override restores a prior configuration.

states.description: Substates within a compound state (Kernel §4.3). REQUIRED (non-empty) when `type` = 'compound'; MUST NOT appear on atomic, parallel, or final states (enforced structurally by the allOf block on this State). The compound state's `initialState` MUST match a key in this map.

regions.description: Named concurrent regions within a parallel state (Kernel §4.3, §4.4, §4.8). REQUIRED (non-empty) when `type` = 'parallel'; MUST NOT appear on atomic, compound, or final states (enforced structurally by the allOf block on this State). Each region executes concurrently; the parallel state exits per the `cancellationPolicy`.

cancellationPolicy.description: Cancellation policy for parallel states (Kernel §4.4). 'wait-all': exit when all regions reach final state (default). 'cancel-siblings': exit when any region reaches final, cancel others. 'fail-fast': exit immediately on any error final state. Only permitted when type = 'parallel'; enforced structurally by the allOf block on this State.

historyState.description: History state mode for compound states (Kernel §4.14). When present, overrides initialState on reentry: 'shallow' resumes the last active direct substate, 'deep' restores the full nested configuration. Semantics defined in the Lifecycle Detail Companion (S3). Only permitted when type = 'compound'; enforced structurally by the allOf block on this State.

description.description: Free-text description of this state's purpose within the workflow (e.g., what the case is waiting for, which actors act here). Consumed by documentation and audit tooling; not interpreted by the processor.
examples: Awaiting manager approval of the submitted purchase order
Final state reached when the applicant has been found eligible

initialState.description: Identifier of the state where this parallel region begins execution when the enclosing parallel state is entered (Kernel §4.4, §4.8). MUST match a key in this region's sibling `states` map.

states.description: States contained within this parallel region (Kernel §4.4). Each region carries its own independent statechart that runs concurrently with sibling regions. The region exits when it reaches a final state; the overall parallel state exits per its `cancellationPolicy`.

event.description: Identifier of the event that triggers this transition (Kernel §4.5, §4.6). When an event of this name arrives for an active state that owns this transition, the processor evaluates the (optional) guard; the first transition whose guard evaluates true in document order fires. Names prefixed with `$` are reserved for kernel-generated events (`$join`, `$timeout.*`, `$error`, `$related.*`) per Kernel §4.10 — workflow authors MUST NOT define `$`-prefixed event names.

x-lm.intent: The event that triggers this transition

target.description: Identifier of the destination state for this transition. MUST name a state declared somewhere in the lifecycle's `states` map (or a substate reachable by dotted path where the binding supports it). When the transition fires, the processor runs the source state's `onExit` actions, the transition's own `actions`, and the target state's `onEntry` actions in that order (Kernel §4.7). A Kernel Complete processor rejects undeclared targets (§13.3).

x-lm.intent: The destination state when this transition fires

guard.description: FEL expression that must evaluate to true for the transition to fire. Evaluated in the evaluation context (Kernel S7).

actions.description: Actions executed during the transition, after source onExit and before target onEntry.

items.description: One semantic tag identifier on this transition. Tags are free-form lowercase strings used by governance layers to attach rules via the `lifecycleHook` seam (Kernel §4.12). Conventionally recognized values include `determination`, `review`, `adverse-decision`, `quality-check`, `intake`, `appeal`, and `notification`.

tags.description: Semantic tags for governance attachment via lifecycleHook (Kernel §4.12).

description.description: Free-text explanation of this transition (e.g., which business condition it represents, who triggers it). Consumed by documentation and audit tooling; not interpreted by the processor.
examples: Manager approves the purchase order (under $50k threshold)
Applicant withdraws the application before a decision is issued

x-lm.intent: Determines what operation is performed at this step

taskRef.description: Reference to a task definition. Used with createTask.

assignTo.description: Actor identifier to assign the task to. Used with createTask.

serviceRef.description: Reference to an external service. Used with invokeService.

idempotencyKey.description: Idempotency key for deduplicating service invocations (Kernel S9.3). Closes the crash-between-invoke-and-persist window.

correlationKey.description: Correlation key for routing external callbacks to the correct workflow instance (Kernel S9.4).

path.description: Case file field path. Used with setData.

value.description: Value to set. Used with setData. May be a literal or FEL expression string.

eventType.description: Event type identifier. Used with emitEvent.

data.description: Event data payload. Used with emitEvent.

timerId.description: Timer identifier. Used with startTimer and cancelTimer.

deadline.description: ISO 8601 datetime deadline. Used with startTimer and createTask.

event.description: Event to emit when timer fires. Used with startTimer.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this action does.

compensatingAction.description: Semantically meaningful reversal action (Kernel S9.5). The compensation seam only -- detailed execution semantics deferred to Lifecycle Detail companion.

contractRef.description: Reference to a contract in the contracts map for validation or Formspec-backed task presentation.

prefillMappingRef.description: Mapping document used to prefill a Formspec-backed task response. Used with createTask; overrides the ContractReference value for this task.

responseMappingRef.description: Mapping document used to project a completed Formspec Response into case state. Used with createTask; overrides the ContractReference value for this task.

completionEvent.description: Event emitted when a Formspec-backed task completes successfully.

failureEvent.description: Event emitted when a Formspec-backed task fails validation, ledger gating, abandonment, or post-pass checks.

condition.description: FEL expression evaluated against case state. When true, the milestone is reached.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this milestone represents.

fields.description: Map of field names to FieldDefinition entries. Each entry defines one typed data slot in the case file — a named column in the case's typed data schema. Fields are append-only at the value level: every mutation is recorded in the case state history (Kernel §5.4), never overwritten in place. Field names MUST be unique within the case file; FEL expressions address them as `caseFile.<fieldName>` (Kernel §7.2).

x-lm.intent: Defines the shape of case data that accumulates during workflow execution

relationships.description: Typed relationships to other case instances (Kernel S5.5). Relationships are metadata -- they do NOT affect lifecycle evaluation. Cross-case behavioral interaction uses correlationKey (Kernel S9.4).

type.description: Classifier for this cross-case relationship (Kernel §5.5). Standard values: `parent` (this case is a child of the target), `child` (this case is a parent of the target), `sibling` (peer cases under a shared parent), `related` (generic association), and `supersedes` (this case replaces the target). The type is extensible via `x-`-prefixed vendor values; processors MUST ignore unknown `x-` types for forward compatibility. Relationships are metadata — they do NOT affect lifecycle evaluation.

x-lm.intent: Classifies the nature of the cross-case relationship

targetCase.description: URI reference to the related case instance. Typically a URN under the authoring organization's namespace; any resolvable URI scheme is permitted. The target case MAY reside in the same WOS deployment or in a federated one.

relationship.description: Free-text semantic label describing the nature of the relationship beyond the coarse `type` classifier. Useful for human-oriented audit views and for domain-specific relationship taxonomies.

bidirectional.description: When `true`, the target case SHOULD also record the inverse relationship so the two cases are mutually discoverable. The kernel does not enforce the inverse link — it is an authoring guidance flag for binding implementations and tooling.

type.description: Primitive data type of this case-file field (Kernel §5.3). Used for structural validation of case-state values and for type-aware FEL evaluation. `date` and `datetime` carry ISO 8601 values; `object` and `array` are structurally unconstrained at the kernel layer — richer contracts attach via the contract mechanism (§11).

description.description: Free-text description of what this field represents (e.g., the source of its data, its units, who is expected to populate it). Consumed by documentation and authoring tooling; not interpreted by the processor.
examples: Applicant's annual gross income in USD
Date the applicant signed the form

default.description: Default value assigned to this field when a case instance is created and the field has not yet been explicitly set. If omitted, the field begins unset (null under FEL reads). The value MUST be consistent with the declared `type`.

binding.description: Contract binding type (Kernel S11.2). 'formspec': Formspec Definition (recommended) -- full reactive behavior, cross-field Shapes, Mapping DSL. 'jsonSchema': JSON Schema (baseline) -- structural validation only.

x-lm.intent: Determines the validation capabilities and processing delegation model

ref.description: URI reference to the contract document (Formspec Definition or JSON Schema).

description.description: Human-readable description of this contract's purpose.

prefillMappingRef.description: Mapping document used to prefill Formspec-backed tasks that use this contract. Prefer the same bidirectional Mapping document as responseMappingRef when the host supports Mapping Bidirectional.

responseMappingRef.description: Mapping document used to project completed Formspec Responses into WOS case state. If absent, Runtime Companion S15 forbids automatic host-defined Response-to-case projection.

digestAlgorithm.description: Cryptographic digest algorithm for input/output tamper detection (Kernel S8.3). Implementation-defined but recorded here for auditability.

workflowTimeout.description: Maximum total workflow duration as ISO 8601 duration (Kernel S9.7).

defaultTaskTimeout.description: Default timeout for human tasks as ISO 8601 duration.

defaultServiceTimeout.description: Default timeout for external service invocations as ISO 8601 duration.

compensable.description: When true, the workflow scope is compensable (Kernel S9.5). Enables the compensation seam.

instanceVersioning.description: Instance versioning policy (Kernel S9.6). 'pinned': instance bound to creation-time definition version. 'migrateable': instance may be explicitly migrated to a newer version.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
